hypothesis: Incorporating multi-scale SEM imaging data (e.g., combining 500×, 2000×, and 5000× magnification images of the same cathode sample) as a multi-resolution input to the two-stage transfer learning framework will yield significantly higher classification accuracy for intermediate aging states (formation-aged samples) compared to single-magnification inputs, because particle-level cracking features that are ambiguous at low magnification become discriminative at higher magnifications, thereby reducing the inter-class confusion between formation-aged and 100-cycle classes that persists in the current single-magnification framework.
